systems.name: Harmonic Substitution Lattice
systems.summary: The book's central system: a graph of diatonic and non-diatonic arpeggios organized by their shared-tone relationships to underlying chord tones. Members are the substitution arpeggio types available over each chord quality. Traversal rules govern moves between arpeggios within a bar or across changes — primarily by common tone, guide-tone preservation, or root-interval relationship (3rd, 5th, 7th). Modification rules govern how a given arpeggio is altered: shell-voicing reduction, reframing pentatonics as extended arpeggios, scale rerooting for non-diatonic options, chord-tone omission, and avoidance of unintended sus4 colors. Established in Chapter 1, this system is the lens through which the entire book operates.

traversal_rules.name: Guide-Tone Arpeggio Selection
traversal_rules.summary: Select the substitute arpeggio whose notes contain the chord's defining 3rd and 7th intervals.

references.topic: Identifying guide tones of altered dominant
references.quote_excerpt: defining tones of a Bb7alt chord are the 3rd and 7th (F and Ab)

traversal_rules.name: Shared-Tone Arpeggio Move
traversal_rules.summary: Move to a substitute arpeggio by maximizing notes held in common with the underlying chord.

references.quote_excerpt: Fmaj7 and Am7 have three notes in common

traversal_rules.name: Arpeggio Root Hierarchy (3rd > 5th > 7th)
traversal_rules.summary: Prefer arpeggio rooted on the chord's 3rd; treat 5th- and 7th-rooted options with increasing caution.

references.quote_excerpt: arpeggios from the higher notes (5th and 7th), you include fewer notes
playing a m7b5 chord on the 3rd of a dominant 7 chord

traversal_rules.name: 5th-of-II Arpeggio Targets 3rd of V
traversal_rules.summary: Arpeggiate from the 5th of the II chord to voice-lead toward the 3rd of the following V chord.

references.topic: 5th of II chord targets 3rd of V
references.quote_excerpt: Playing an arpeggio from the 5th of the II chord

traversal_rules.name: Tritone Substitute Traversal
traversal_rules.summary: Replace an altered dominant arpeggio with the arpeggio of its tritone substitute, preserving guide tones.

references.quote_excerpt: E7 is the tritone substitute of Bb7

modification_rules.name: Shell Voicing Reduction
modification_rules.summary: Reduce any chord to root, 3rd, and 7th only to create a spare melodic skeleton.

references.quote_excerpt: shell voicing is a chord voicing that use only the root, 3rd and 7th

modification_rules.name: Pentatonic Reframe as Extended Arpeggio
modification_rules.summary: Reinterpret a pentatonic scale as a named extended arpeggio to make its harmonic function explicit.

references.quote_excerpt: notes of D Minor Pentatonic as though they are an arpeggio and they spell a Dm7add11

modification_rules.name: Non-Diatonic Arpeggio Construction by Scale Rerooting
modification_rules.summary: Derive non-diatonic arpeggios by reading scale tones starting from an alternate root degree.

references.quote_excerpt: construct this arpeggio with notes from the scale

modification_rules.name: Chord Tone Omission for Open Sound
modification_rules.summary: Omit a defining chord tone (e.g. the 3rd) to create an open, ambiguous line acceptable within a clear harmonic context.

references.topic: omitting chord tone for open sound
references.quote_excerpt: omitted the 3rd (Bb) of Gm7 which immediately makes the line more open sounding

modification_rules.name: Sus4 Tension Avoidance over Dominant
modification_rules.summary: Avoid the 11th (C over G7) when an unwanted sus4 color is undesirable; deploy only with intent.

references.quote_excerpt: Playing a C over a G7 chord will suggest a G7sus4 sound

systems.name: Chromatic Enclosure System
systems.summary: Governs how non-chord chromatic and diatonic passing tones are organized around target chord tones to produce characteristic bebop and hardbop melodic language. Members are enclosure construction types and resolution placement positions. Traversal rules specify where chromatic notes land in the rhythmic grid (off-beats) and how the improviser moves from one enclosure to the next target note. Modification rules govern when a diatonic substitute replaces a chromatic note and how beat-2 placement displaces resolution to beat 4 for a modern unresolved effect.

members.name: Diatonic Note Substitution for Missing Half-Step

traversal_rules.name: Chromatic Notes on Off-Beats
traversal_rules.summary: Place all chromatic passing notes on off-beats, keeping strong chord tones on the beat.

references.quote_excerpt: chromatic notes are generally placed on the off-beats

traversal_rules.name: Enclosure Must Target Chord / Scale Tone
traversal_rules.summary: Every enclosure must resolve to a chord or scale tone; enclosures floating free of harmonic targets are forbidden.

references.quote_excerpt: main purpose is to target or gravitate towards scale / chord tones

traversal_rules.name: Beat-2 Enclosure Displacement
traversal_rules.summary: Place an enclosure on beat 2 so it resolves on beat 4, producing a Metheny-esque unresolved sound.

references.quote_excerpt: placed on beat 2, so that it will resolve on beat 4

traversal_rules.name: Legato Technique for Chromatic Softening
traversal_rules.summary: Use hammer-ons, pull-offs, and slides so chromatic passing notes remain soft while scale tones are accented.

references.quote_excerpt: passing note is accented and the scale note is softer

modification_rules.name: Diatonic Gap Fill When Half-Step Absent
modification_rules.summary: When a chromatic half-step gap does not exist between two enclosure notes, substitute the diatonic note above.

references.topic: diatonic note substitution for chromatic gap
references.quote_excerpt: not possible to add a chromatic note in between, I added a G note

modification_rules.name: Four-Note Enclosure: Suspension vs. Tension-Pull
modification_rules.summary: Deploy a four-note enclosure either to create a suspended sound preceding a chord tone, or as a tension-and-pull device driving toward resolution.

references.quote_excerpt: precede a chord tone to create a suspended sound, or they can be used as a way of

systems.name: Diminished Symmetry Triad System
systems.summary: Exploits the half-whole diminished scale's minor-third symmetry to generate the four embedded major triads and deploy them as upper-structure melodic material over dominant 7th chords (notably the 13b9 voicing). Members are the four major triads and their inversions. Traversal rules govern common-tone voice-leading, encirclement, and positional cycling. Modification rules apply the minor-third symmetry axiom to transpose any shape by m3, select triad pairs for specific extensions, and bridge to the altered scale via the B major triad.

traversal_rules.name: Minor-Third Symmetry Rotation
traversal_rules.summary: Transpose any triad shape or arpeggio by minor thirds to access all four positions within the diminished scale.

references.quote_excerpt: Half Whole Diminished scale can be moved in minor 3rds

traversal_rules.name: Common-Tone Triad Chaining
traversal_rules.summary: Connect consecutive triads by linking shared top or bottom notes across the triad boundary.

references.quote_excerpt: linking the top two notes of the D triad to the lowest two notes of the Ab triad

traversal_rules.name: Encirclement as Triad-to-Triad Transition
traversal_rules.summary: Use the last two notes of one triad to encircle the first note of the next triad.

references.quote_excerpt: last two notes of one triad to encircle the first note of the next

traversal_rules.name: Positional Cycling Before Neck Cross
traversal_rules.summary: Cycle through all four triad inversions in a single neck position before crossing to the next position.

references.topic: Cycling Triads in Position Up the Neck
references.quote_excerpt: cycling through the triads in a single position before moving to the next position

modification_rules.name: Color-Tone Triad Pair Selection
modification_rules.summary: Select the Ab+D triad pair to express b9+#9+13 color over F7; use B+Ab pair for #11+b9+#9 color.

references.topic: Ab and D Triads Conveying F7 Extensions
references.quote_excerpt: Ab and D major triads conveys the sound of an F7

references.topic: B and Ab triads — b9 #9 #11 colour
references.quote_excerpt: B and Ab major triads … b9, #9 and #11

modification_rules.name: Root-Position Triad Omission for Colour
modification_rules.summary: Omit the F major triad (root upper-structure) because it adds no harmonic colour over F7.

references.quote_excerpt: don't often use the F major triad because it doesn't add any colour

modification_rules.name: Altered-Diminished Bridge via B Major Triad
modification_rules.summary: Use the B major triad to bridge from diminished to altered territory, since it is shared by both scales.

references.topic: B Major Triad Intervals Against F Root
references.quote_excerpt: B major triad is not unique to the diminished sound

systems.name: Melodic Line Construction System
systems.summary: A system for assembling complete jazz improvisation lines from named melodic gestures: ascending arpeggio + descending scale (the master's signature shape), Coltrane 1-2-3-5 and 1-b3-4-5 groupings, motif repetition with alteration, and the counterpoint rule that large intervals resolve stepwise in the opposite direction. Traversal rules specify gesture-to-gesture connections; modification rules govern rhythmic subdivision variation, scale-tone interpolation, and motivic alteration. This system wraps the harmonic substitution lattice and chromatic enclosure systems in a procedural envelope.

traversal_rules.name: Target-Note Approach
traversal_rules.summary: Organize the line by working backward from a specific chord tone to reach on the next strong beat.

references.quote_excerpt: both motif-based and target note ideas in your arsenal

traversal_rules.name: Motif Sequencing Across Chord Changes
traversal_rules.summary: Carry a rhythmic or intervallic motif across a chord change by restating it on the new harmony's arpeggio.

references.quote_excerpt: descending Bbmaj7 arpeggio motif is repeated using the descending E diminished arpeggio

traversal_rules.name: Counterpoint Large-Interval Stepwise Resolution
traversal_rules.summary: After a large ascending interval, resolve the line stepwise in the opposite (descending) direction.

references.topic: Counterpoint rule: large intervals resolve stepwise
references.quote_excerpt: large ascending intervals with a stepwise movement in the opposite direction
tension of the large ascending interval is resolved with step-wise motion

traversal_rules.name: Harmonic Minor Dominant Pull
traversal_rules.summary: Over the V chord, apply harmonic minor material to create a pull toward the next tonic chord.

references.quote_excerpt: harmonic minor on Bb7 to pull towards the Eb7

modification_rules.name: Melodic Repeat with Slight Alteration to Fit New Harmony
modification_rules.summary: Restate a melodic phrase on a new chord, changing only one or two notes to align with the new harmony.

references.quote_excerpt: repeats it over the Edim chord, altering it slightly to fit

modification_rules.name: Rhythmic Subdivision Embellishment
modification_rules.summary: Introduce triplet or sixteenth-note subdivisions within an eighth-note arpeggio line to vary phrasing and imply passing chords.

references.topic: Rhythmic variation with triplet passing notes
references.quote_excerpt: 8th note triplet is used for the chromatic passing notes

modification_rules.name: Scale-Tone Interpolation to Avoid Predictability
modification_rules.summary: Add scale tones between arpeggio notes while ensuring chord tones still land on the beat.

references.quote_excerpt: Embellishing an arpeggio by adding scale notes in between

modification_rules.name: Coltrane Pattern Minor Variant (1-b3-4-5)
modification_rules.summary: On minor chords, apply the 1-b3-4-5 variant of the Coltrane pattern to break up scalic lines.

references.topic: Chapter assignments: II V I lick writing
references.quote_excerpt: Coltrane patterns (1 2 35 on a major chord, 1 b3 4.5 on a minor chord)
Coltrane patterns are a useful way to break up scalic lines
